# Personalize outreach emails generated by the Sales Qualification Agent

The Sales Qualification Agent (SQA) generates outreach emails to engage prospects and qualify leads. As an administrator, you can personalize these emails to align with your organization's brand voice, outreach scenarios, and messaging preferences. Personalized outreach drives higher response rates and improves lead qualification by making communication feel intentional and contextual rather than generic.

All personalization settings are optional. If you don't configure any settings, the agent continues to generate outreach emails using its default cold-prospecting behavior. You can progressively add sophistication over time without disrupting existing workflows.

## What you can personalize

You can configure the following aspects of outreach emails generated by the Sales Qualification Agent:

| Area | What you can control |
|---|---|
| **Outreach scenario** | The purpose or goal of the email (for example, trial ending, event follow-up, re-engagement) |
| **Call-to-action** | The desired next step for the recipient (for example, reply, book a meeting, request an intro) |
| **Subject line** | A fixed suggestion or style recommendation for subject lines |
| **Tone and voice** | Brand-aligned tone guidance, name-usage limits, and preferred terminology |
| **Brand language** | Approved phrases, banned words, and voice guardrails |
| **Message length** | Brevity targets and word count preferences |
| **Formatting** | Bullet vs. paragraph style, paragraph length, and maximum bullet count |

## Configure outreach email personalization

1. In Sales Hub, go to **App Settings** > **Sales Qualification Agent settings** > **Email instructions**.
1. Select the **AI Personalization** option.
1. In the **Email guidance** field, enter your personalization instructions in natural language.

### Define the outreach scenario

Specify the purpose of outreach so that emails lead with the reason for contact rather than generic messaging.

Include in your email guidance:
- The goal of the outreach (for example, "Follow up with prospects who attended our webinar last week")
- Re-engagement scenarios (for example, "Reach out to customers whose trial is ending in 14 days")
- Specific audiences (for example, "Website visitors who downloaded our whitepaper")

> [!TIP]
> The outreach scenario drives the opening of the email. When specified, the agent structures the message to lead with the reason for outreach, followed by supporting personalization, value proposition, and the call-to-action.

### Configure the call-to-action

Define the desired next step so the agent generates an appropriate call-to-action (CTA) for each email.

In your email guidance, specify:
- The CTA type (for example, "Reply to the email," "Book a meeting," "Request an introduction")
- The level of assertiveness: suggestive or action-oriented

### Provide subject line guidance

Guide how the agent crafts email subject lines to improve open rates.

In your email guidance, include:
- A specific subject line for the agent to use or closely follow, or
- Style recommendations (for example, "Keep subject lines under 8 words and use curiosity-driven language")

### Set tone and voice preferences

Provide descriptive guidance to ensure outreach emails reflect your company's brand voice and the seller's communication style.

In your email guidance, include:
- Tone guidance (for example, "Calm, commercially sharp, no overselling" or "Professional but approachable, avoid jargon")
- Name-usage limits (for example, "Don't repeat the company name more than once")
- Preferred terminology (for example, "Use 'solution' instead of 'product'")

> [!NOTE]
> Tone guidance is provided as text instructions. You don't need to upload example emails or create complex playbooks.

### Configure brand language guardrails

Define approved phrases and banned words to protect brand consistency across all outreach emails.

In your email guidance, list:
- **Approved phrases** that the agent should use when relevant (for example, your value proposition tagline or product names)
- **Banned words** or phrases that must not appear in outreach emails (for example, competitor names or outdated terminology)

### Set message length and formatting preferences

Control the length and structure of outreach emails to improve readability and engagement.

In your email guidance, specify:
- **Message length**: Word count target (for example, 100–150 words)
- **Format style**: Preference for bullets, paragraphs, or a combination
- **Paragraph length**: Brevity targets (for example, "No more than 3 sentences per paragraph")
- **Maximum bullet count**: Limit on bullet points per email

These are preferences that guide the agent's output. The agent uses them as targets rather than rigid constraints.

## How defaults work

The outreach personalization settings are designed for low setup friction. The following defaults apply:

| Setting | Default behavior when not configured |
|---|---|
| Outreach scenario | Cold-prospecting outreach to new prospects |
| Call-to-action | Agent-generated CTA based on context |
| Subject line | Agent-generated subject line |
| Tone and voice | Neutral, professional AI tone |
| Brand language | No guardrails applied |
| Message length | Standard agent-generated length |
| Formatting | Agent-determined structure |

When you provide scenario instructions or custom guidance, the agent prioritizes your configured scenario, account research, and value proposition to drive personalization.

## Example email guidance

### Trial ending scenario

```
Outreach scenario: Follow up with customers whose trial is ending in 14 days to prevent churn and encourage conversion to a paid plan.

Call-to-action: Book a meeting with our sales team to discuss pricing and implementation options.

Subject line: Keep subject lines under 7 words. Use urgency-driven language like "Your trial ends soon" or "Upgrade before [Date]."

Tone and voice: Helpful and supportive, not pushy. Acknowledge the trial experience first. Use "customer" instead of "user." Avoid repeating the product name more than once.

Brand language: Use our core value proposition "Streamline [function] and scale faster." Avoid competitor names and outdated product terminology.

Message length: 80–120 words.

Formatting: Use short paragraphs (2–3 sentences max). Prefer 1–2 bullet points if highlighting key benefits.
```

### Event follow-up scenario

```
Outreach scenario: Follow up with prospects who attended our webinar or conference to continue the conversation and move them toward a demo.

Call-to-action: Schedule a 20-minute demo to see how our solution addresses their specific use case.

Subject line: Reference the event name or topic from the event they attended. Keep under 8 words (for example, "Quick follow-up from [Event Name]").

Tone and voice: Warm and conversational, showing genuine interest in their feedback. Use first names. Avoid corporate jargon.

Brand language: Use "solution" instead of "product." Include our tagline "Built for modern teams" when relevant. Don't mention competitors.

Message length: 90–130 words.

Formatting: Use paragraphs primarily. Include 1 bullet point maximum to highlight the demo offer.
```
